_comment: BEYOND-DUCKLING extension. `<number>-<unit>` hyphenated forms ('a 3-inch pipe', 'an 8-ounce glass') are common in real English/speech but upstream Duckling does NOT parse them (verified against the oracle: '3-inch'/'six-mile' -> nothing). The distance cases are attested in Microsoft Recognizers-Text's Dimension specs; volume/quantity/temperature are analogous. Each dimension's parser must resolve the hyphenated form exactly like its spaced equivalent. Values compared with tolerance (reuses approx_eq). This is an intentional coverage extension for the speech product, kept additive so the Duckling corpus stays 100% green.
